description:

The California primary is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026.

If no nominees are announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.sos.ca.gov/.

No major scandals or shifts reported, though early voting is underway ahead of the March 5 Super Tuesday contest; low-turnout primaries often favor incumbents, tempering upset risks but highlighting potential for the second-place finisher to face Khanna in November.

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Persistent Iran-Israel tensions, capped by Tehran's seizure of the MSC Aries container ship on April 13 amid retaliatory strikes, form the primary driver behind the 66.5% market-implied probability for "No" on Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizing by April's end. Trader consensus reflects sustained caution, with tanker transits down 15-20% from March averages per AIS data, fueling spikes in war risk insurance premiums to $50,000+ per voyage and VLCC charter rates nearing $100,000/day. This embeds a geopolitical risk premium into Brent crude forwards, hovering near $90/bbl, as OPEC+ Gulf exporters face delays. Key catalysts include potential U.S. naval escalations or Iranian proxy flare-ups before April 30 resolution, outweighing de-escalation hopes.

question: Will Arsenal reach the UEFA Champions League semifinal?
description:

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to advance to the UEFA Champions League semifinal (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve to the country that receives the highest number of points from the professional juries in the Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Eurovision 2026 Jury Winner tilts toward France at 26.5% implied probability, nipping at Australia's heels with 24%, fueled by both nations' track records of top jury finishes—France's elegant ballads and Australia's polished pop consistently impressing professional panels over the past five years. Finland's 18.5% surge stems from Käärijä's 2023 televote dominance spilling into jury optimism, while Denmark's 14% underscores Nordic delegation prowess. In this razor-thin race among perennial powerhouses, key differentiators include upcoming national selection reveals favoring jury-preferred sophistication over gimmicks, with odds volatile until early 2026 entries emerge post-2025 contest.

question: Will fewer than 10 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between March 10-16?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from March 10, 2026 through March 16, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for March 16, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

series.title: ships transit the Strait of Hormuz

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors 35-39 ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz during March 10-16, capturing 75.5% implied probability, with 40-44 a distant second at 16.3%, based on consistent historical weekly averages of 35-40 oil tankers amid stable Persian Gulf shipping lanes. Recent U.S. Energy Information Administration data shows 37 transits the prior week (March 3-9), aligning with steady volumes despite Houthi disruptions elsewhere in the Middle East diverting minimal Hormuz traffic. No new Iranian seizures or blockades reported this week, and routine U.S. naval patrols maintain open passage, supporting expectations of normal crude and LNG flows without escalation catalysts on the horizon.

question: Ethereal FDV above $50M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Ethereal's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ethereal (https://x.com/etherealdex) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will FC Hradec Králové win on 2026-04-05?

In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 5, 2026
If FC Hradec Králové wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
